name: Daemon Commit Logic

tasks.title: Refactor autoCommitPartialWork into a general daemon commit function
tasks.description: Refactor autoCommitPartialWork into a general daemon commit function
tasks.body:
Refactor the existing autoCommitPartialWork function in internal/daemon/iteration.go into a more general commitAfterIteration function that handles both success and failure paths.

The new function should:
1. Accept a commit type (success or failure) and the task ID
2. Check the relevant config flag (commit_on_success or commit_on_failure) before proceeding
3. Respect the auto_commit master switch
4. Respect the commit_state flag when staging (if false, do not stage .wolfcastle/)
5. Use appropriate commit messages per the spec:
   - Success: 'wolfcastle: <task-id> complete'
   - Failure: 'wolfcastle: <task-id> partial (attempt <N>)'
6. Skip the commit if the working tree is clean after staging

The existing autoCommitPartialWork call site (failure path, around line 384) should be updated to call the new function. The success path (around lines 274-373) should also call it after state updates.

Validate task ID format before embedding in commit message (existing pattern).
acceptance_criteria: Both success and failure paths call the commit function

tasks.title: Implement staging area preservation
tasks.body:
Implement staging area preservation so the daemon's git add/commit cycle does not clobber the user's manually staged changes.

The spec suggests two approaches:
1. Stash-based: git stash --keep-index before commit, git stash pop after
2. Separate GIT_INDEX_FILE: use a temporary index file for daemon commits, leaving user's index untouched

Evaluate both approaches and choose the more reliable one. The separate GIT_INDEX_FILE approach is likely more robust because stash can conflict with existing stashes or fail on edge cases.

Implementation should wrap the commit function from task-0001: save user index state before staging, restore after commit. If the preservation mechanism fails, log a warning but do not block the commit (the commit itself is more important than preserving staged state).

Write tests that verify:
- User's staged changes survive a daemon commit
- User's unstaged changes are not affected
- The preservation mechanism handles clean working trees gracefully
constraints: Do not break existing autoCommitPartialWork behavior while adding preservation
acceptance_criteria: User staging area is preserved across daemon commits
Tests verify staged changes survive daemon commits

tasks.title: Update iteration tests for new commit behavior
tasks.description: Update iteration tests for new commit behavior
tasks.body:
Update existing tests in the iteration test file to account for the new commit behavior:

1. Tests that previously asserted on agent commit behavior need updating (the agent no longer commits)
2. Add tests for the success-path commit:
   - Verify commit happens after successful task completion when commit_on_success is true
   - Verify no commit when commit_on_success is false
   - Verify no commit when auto_commit is false
3. Update failure-path tests:
   - Verify commit happens when commit_on_failure is true (existing behavior, new config)
   - Verify no commit when commit_on_failure is false
4. Test commit_state flag:
   - When true, .wolfcastle/ is staged
   - When false, only code changes are staged
5. Test clean working tree skip (no empty commits)
6. Verify commit message format matches spec for both paths
acceptance_criteria: All new config flag combinations are tested
Success and failure paths both have commit test coverage

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Daemon Commit Logic is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify success path in iteration.go calls the commit function
Verify staging area preservation does not clobber user's git index

breadcrumbs.text: Refactored autoCommitPartialWork into commitAfterIteration: now handles both success and failure paths, respects auto_commit/commit_on_success/commit_on_failure/commit_state config flags, uses spec-compliant commit messages

Implemented staging area preservation using separate GIT_INDEX_FILE in commitWithSeparateIndex. Temp index seeded from HEAD, staged into, committed from. User's .git/index never touched. Falls back to commitDirect on failure. Four new tests verify byte-level index preservation, working tree safety, cleanup, and clean-tree handling.

Created iteration_test.go with 14 tests covering commitAfterIteration flag combinations (auto_commit, commit_on_success, commit_on_failure, commit_state disabled/enabled, invalid task ID) and runIteration integration tests verifying success-path commits, failure-path commits, disabled-flag skips, and clean-tree no-empty-commit behavior.

task-0001.objective: Refactor autoCommitPartialWork into a general commitAfterIteration function that handles both success and failure commit paths with config-driven behavior.
task-0001.what_happened: Replaced autoCommitPartialWork with commitAfterIteration in internal/daemon/iteration.go. The new function accepts a commit kind (success/failure), attempt number, and the full GitConfig struct. It checks auto_commit, commit_on_success, commit_on_failure, and commit_state before proceeding. Commit messages now follow the spec: 'wolfcastle: <task-id> complete' for success, 'wolfcastle: <task-id> partial (attempt <N>)' for failure. Updated the failure call site to compute attemptNum from FailureCount+1, and added a success call site after the COMPLETE marker's state propagation. Updated all tests in iteration_coverage_test.go.
went_well: Config flag gating is clean: a switch on kind selects the right boolean, the master switch short-circuits everything.
Kept commitAfterIteration as a free function (not a Daemon method) so existing tests remain lightweight without needing a full Daemon instance.
improvements: The function still shells out to git commands directly; a future pass could route through the git.Provider interface for better testability.
action_items: Add tests for the new config flag behaviors (auto_commit=false, commit_on_success=false, commit_on_failure=false, commit_state=false).
Add a test for the success commit path (kind='success') to verify the commit message format.

task-0002.objective: Implement staging area preservation so daemon commits don't clobber user-staged changes
task-0002.what_happened: Chose the separate GIT_INDEX_FILE approach over stash-based. Added commitWithSeparateIndex which creates a temp index seeded from HEAD via git read-tree, stages tracked changes and .wolfcastle/ state into it, then commits from it. The user's .git/index is never read or written. Added commitDirect as a fallback when temp index creation fails. Updated the existing MultipleTrackedFiles test (was asserting clean working tree via git status, but with separate index the default index is stale relative to the new HEAD). Added four new tests: PreservesUserStagedChanges (verifies .git/index is byte-identical before/after), PreservesUserUnstagedChanges (working tree content unchanged), CleanTreeNoCommit (no commit on clean tree), TempFileCleanedUp (no leftover .git-daemon-index-* files).
went_well: The separate-index approach is clean: zero interaction with .git/index, no stash edge cases, no pop conflicts
Byte-level index comparison in tests gives strong assurance that user state is truly untouched
Fallback to commitDirect ensures commits still land even if the preservation mechanism breaks
improvements: After a separate-index commit, git status from the user's perspective shows stale entries because the default index references the pre-commit tree. This is cosmetically confusing but data-safe. A future enhancement could reconcile the default index after commit.
action_items: Consider documenting the stale-index behavior for users who run git status during daemon execution

task-0003.objective: Add tests covering all commit config flag combinations and integration with runIteration
task-0003.what_happened: Created internal/daemon/iteration_test.go with 14 new tests. Seven unit tests exercise commitAfterIteration directly with disabled flags (auto_commit, commit_on_success, commit_on_failure), enabled success path, failure message format, commit_state true/false, and invalid task ID rejection. Seven integration tests run runIteration through a real git repo to verify success-path commits, failure-path commits, disabled-flag skips, auto_commit master switch, and clean-tree no-empty-commit behavior.
went_well: Table-driven approach not needed here since each flag combination has distinct setup; individual named tests read clearly in go test -v output
Reused initGitRepo and testGitCfg helpers from iteration_coverage_test.go; added modifyTrackedFile, gitRunEnv, gitLog, iterTestLogger helpers to reduce boilerplate
improvements: The commit_state disabled test relies on .wolfcastle/ being untracked to prove exclusion; a tracked .wolfcastle/ file modified then excluded would be a stronger assertion but the current git add -u plus separate index makes that scenario complex
action_items: No further test gaps identified for the commit logic; coverage of edge cases like concurrent commits or SIGTERM during commit is out of scope for unit tests
